user_message: Just got this from Michael Petrov at Humongous Insurance -- I need help figuring out renewal risk. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 16:46 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 11:21 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Humongous Insurance renewal risk

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 10:07 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-K trap (Strict read-only when fast MSX risk-flag update would matter); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-K failure mode (Strict read-only when fast MSX risk-flag update would matter)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-K -- Strict read-only when fast MSX risk-flag update would matter
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
